content.claim: School-district authorities recommended entrance-exam score thresholds of 70 points (out of stated scale) for admission to six- and eight-year gymnasia in 2024/25, with parents' advocacy groups arguing this could reduce available places and push students involuntarily into vocational training.
content.context: Fresh live research through the School network planning lens
limitations: The causal inference (thresholds reducing available places) is a contested advocacy claim, not a neutral administrative fact
No independent analysis provided of actual admission outcomes under these thresholds
Unclear whether thresholds represent a new policy change or continuation of prior practice
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
